title: Calycopeplus paucifolius

The classification of Calycopeplus paucifolius is part of the larger plant classification system, which includes the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Celastrales. Further research may be necessary to determine the exact relationships and classification within the plant kingdom.

intro: Calycopeplus paucifolius is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the few-leaved Calycopeplus. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Calycopeplus paucifolius include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
